title: Go 1.26 Toolchain, Programs, Packages, and Evidence · Language Lens: Program Package Main
summary: A small operations team needs a reproducible status tool, but its source, package boundary, formatter, build, runtime, and release identity are unclear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go program package main increment in a reproducible first-program and toolchain evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain how source files, package declarations, imports, init order, and package main produce an executable program.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
